title: Fix serializer method field hidden unique together (#9700)
body:
Fix: SerializerMethodField as HiddenField in unique_together

Corrects an issue where a `SerializerMethodField` that shares its name
with a model field involved in a `unique_together` constraint (or
`UniqueConstraint`) could be incorrectly treated as a `HiddenField`.

This typically occurred if the underlying model field was nullable or had
a default value, and the `SerializerMethodField` was not being correctly
prioritized during the uniqueness metadata processing.

The `ModelSerializer.get_uniqueness_extra_kwargs` method has been updated
to ensure that if a field name corresponding to a model field in a
uniqueness constraint is explicitly declared as a `SerializerMethodField`
on the serializer, it is not converted into a `HiddenField`.

A test case has been added to `tests/test_model_serializer.py`
(`TestSerializerMethodFieldInUniqueTogether`) to verify this behavior.

step_plan.summary: Modify ModelSerializer.get_uniqueness_extra_kwargs to recognize when a field name participating in a unique constraint is explicitly declared as a SerializerMethodField on the serializer, and treat it as present so it is not converted into a HiddenField. Add a test to verify the behavior.

steps.operation: Update uniqueness handling so declared SerializerMethodField instances are treated as present
steps.reason: When computing uniqueness_extra_kwargs and deciding whether to add a HiddenField for a field name participating in a unique_together / UniqueConstraint, the method currently only checks model_fields. If the serializer explicitly declares a SerializerMethodField with the same name, model_fields may not include it and the code will incorrectly add a HiddenField, hiding the declared field. Modify the logic so that when checking presence for each unique constraint name, the code also checks declared_fields and treats declared SerializerMethodField instances as 'present' (e.g., by using: name in declared_fields and isinstance(declared_fields[name], SerializerMethodField)). This ensures the declared SerializerMethodField is preserved and uniqueness_extra_kwargs are set appropriately instead of creating a HiddenField.
steps.side_effects: Tests: Existing tests related to uniqueness handling and HiddenField behavior may need to be updated or will fail until they reflect the new behavior; add the TestSerializerMethodFieldInUniqueTogether unit test to tests/test_model_serializer.py to assert the fix. Dependents: Serializers that previously relied on an implicit HiddenField being inserted for model-backed fields that are nullable/have defaults (and share a name with a declared SerializerMethodField) will now preserve the explicitly declared SerializerMethodField, which is the intended behavior but could change runtime behavior in rare cases where code expected the HiddenField. API: No public API change; behavior is more consistent with explicit declarations. Implementation note: ensure SerializerMethodField is imported or referenced correctly in the module and that the check is implemented in a minimal, well-tested way. Run full test suite to validate no regressions.
